source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/04_2G & 3G (A1417MCW, A1717MCW, F1417MCW)/▶ CE MDR/230706 ★ 1차 제출/A-TCF-5-161 Post-market Surveillance/A-PMS-5-001 Post-market Surveillance Plan (Rev.0).docx

document.title: Post-Market Surveillance Plan for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

sourceSummary.oneLine: Post-market surveillance plan for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW under EU MDR.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Defines PMS data collection, assessment, risk management reassessment, reporting, authority communication, and manufacturer obligations for a Class IIa medical device.
keyPoints: The device is classified as Class IIa according to Regulation (EU) 2017/745 Annex VIII, Rule 17.
The intended purpose is general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children, not mammography.
PMS data sources include literature searches, recall information, similar-device information, customer surveys, feedback, complaints, incidents, and side-effects.
The plan states PMCF study is not required because safety and clinical performance are already known from previous use of the device in 20 years.
PSUR for Class IIa is updated when necessary and at least every two years, and reported when required by the notified body.

concepts.name: Post-market surveillance
concepts.evidence: Post-market surveillance is a set of activities conducted by manufacturers, to collect and evaluate experience gained from medical devices that have been placed on the market, and to identify the need to take any action.

concepts.name: Post-market surveillance plan
concepts.evidence: This document aims to define the post-market surveillance plan (PMSP) of the Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector according to MDR.

concepts.name: Class IIa medical device
concepts.pageTarget: EU MDR Class IIa Medical Device
concepts.evidence: Regulatory classification: Class lla according to Regulation (EU) 2017/745 Annex VIII, Rule 17

concepts.name: Periodic Safety Update Report
concepts.evidence: Our product is Class lla, so we report the PSUR when the certification authority requires it.

concepts.name: Post-market clinical follow-up
concepts.evidence: The PMCF study is not required for digital x-ray flat panel detector, because the safety and clinical performance are already known from previous use of the device in 20 years.

concepts.name: Risk management reassessment
concepts.evidence: Safety-related data in 5. data assessment is carried out according to ISO 14971:2019, clause 5. Risk analysis to 7. Risk control.

concepts.name: Vigilance system

evidence.claim: The document is a Rev.0 Post-Market Surveillance Plan dated December 01, 2022.

evidence.evidenceText: Jurisdictions where the medical device is available: Korea, EU, FDA
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Market Availability

evidence.claim: The expected lifetime of the device is 5 years.
evidence.evidenceText: Expected lifetime of the medical device: 5 years

evidence.claim: The intended purpose is general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children, not mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children... Not to be used for mammography.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Intended Purpose

evidence.claim: The PMS plan is stated to be consistent with ISO 13485 and ISO 14971.
evidence.evidenceText: This post-market surveillance plan is consistent with relevant international standards, in particular ISO 13485 and ISO 14971.

evidence.claim: Applicable EU regulation is Regulation (EU) 2017/745 of 5 April 2017.
evidence.evidenceText: European Union: Regulation (EU) 2017/745 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 5 April 2017

evidence.claim: Relevant standards listed are EN ISO 14971:2019 and EN ISO 13485:2016.
evidence.evidenceText: EN ISO 14971:2019 Medical devices — Application of risk management to medical devices; EN ISO 13485:2016 Medical devices — Quality management systems — Requirements for regulatory purposes

evidence.claim: Relevant guidelines listed include EN ISO/TR 24971:2020, MEDDEV 2.12/1 Rev.8, and ISO/TR 20416:2020.
evidence.evidenceText: Relevant guidelines: EN ISO/TR 24971:2020; MEDDEV 2.12/1 Rev.8; ISO/TR 20416:2020

evidence.claim: PMS data sources include literature searches, recall information, similar-device information, surveys, feedback, complaints, incidents, and side-effects.
evidence.evidenceText: The post-market surveillance data ... is collected as proactive and reactive. Proactive: Literature search; Recall information; Other information about similar devices. Reactive: Information including non-serious incidents and undesirable side-effects... Information, including feedbacks and complaints, provided by users, distributors and importers.

evidence.claim: PMCF study is stated as not required for the digital x-ray flat panel detector, with additional PMCF possible if needed.
evidence.evidenceText: The PMCF study is not required for digital x-ray flat panel detector... if additional PMCF research is needed according to circumstance check, we will proceed the PMCF plan.

evidence.claim: Collected PMS data is assessed using quantitative descriptive statistics and qualitative content analysis.
evidence.evidenceText: Qualitative and quantitative methods are used to assess whether the collected data is related to safety and risk. quantitative methods: descriptive statistics... qualitative methods: content analysis

evidence.claim: Safety-related PMS data is linked to risk management according to ISO 14971:2019 clauses 5 to 7.
evidence.evidenceText: Safety-related data in 5. data assessment is carried out according to ISO 14971:2019, clause 5. Risk analysis to 7. Risk control.

evidence.claim: Preventive and corrective actions are conducted if assessed data cannot be accepted through risk management.
evidence.evidenceText: Preventive actions and corrective actions are conducted if the assessed data cannot be accepted risk through risk management.

evidence.claim: For Class IIa, PSUR is updated when necessary and at least every two years.
evidence.evidenceText: Class IIa: When necessary and at least every two years

evidence.claim: For Class IIa, PSUR is reported when required by the notified body.
evidence.evidenceText: Class IIa... Time to report to competent authorities: Report when required by the notified body; Applicable

evidence.claim: Serious public health threats are reportable immediately and not later than 2 calendar days after manufacturer awareness.
evidence.evidenceText: Serious public health threat: IMMEDIATELY ... but not later than 2 calendar days after awareness by the MANUFACTURER of this threat.

evidence.claim: Death or unanticipated serious deterioration in health is reportable immediately after link establishment and not later than 10 elapsed calendar days after awareness.
evidence.evidenceText: Death or UNANTICIPATED serious deterioration in state of health: IMMEDIATELY ... after the MANUFACTURER established a link between the device and the event but not later than 10 elapsed calendar days following the date of awareness of the event.

evidence.claim: Other reportable events are reportable immediately after link establishment and not later than 30 elapsed calendar days after awareness.
evidence.evidenceText: Others: IMMEDIATELY ... after the MANUFACTURER established a link between the device and the event but not later than 30 elapsed calendar days following the date of awareness of the event.

evidence.claim: Communication with competent authorities follows AQP808 Vigilance System procedure.
evidence.evidenceText: Communication with Competent Authorities follows the AQP808 Vigilance System procedure.

evidence.claim: MDR manufacturer obligations are referenced to Articles 83, 84, and 86.
evidence.evidenceText: Reference to procedures to fulfil the manufacturers obligations laid down in Articles 83, 84 and 86 of the MDR.

reviewFlags.reason: Authority metadata says FDA, but the document content primarily references EU MDR, European Union regulation, notified body, and competent authorities; FDA appears only in the jurisdiction availability list and recall source.
The source does not identify a specific notified body, certification authority, or competent authority name.
Potential template or copy error: the data collection section says 'post-market surveillance data of the stethoscope' although the document product is a Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.
Potential OCR or source quality issues in terms such as 'Class lla', 'Filed Safety Corrective Actions', 'anew risk management report', and inconsistent capitalization of IIa.

Potential internal inconsistency: the document states Class IIa PSUR is applicable and reportable when required by the notified body, but later marks PSUR availability for other than Class III or implantable devices as 'Not Applicable. This is a class IIa medical device.'
